notes_for_human_reviewer: MONOLITHIC-REGISTER FAILURE MODE. The writer has gone all-formal on every piece — including a Slack message with a subject line, 'Dear Mr. Raj' opener, and 'Please advise on your preferred course of action' close. The sponsor note reads correctly; the PM email reads stiff but acceptable; the Slack reads absurdly. Same three-paragraph corporate template applied to all three. Tests register_modulation cap (1) without firing the other caps — hedge density and apology hygiene are actually fine. CALIBRATION NOTE (2026-05-05): first run scored lede_discipline at 1 — all three pieces share the same 'I am writing to inform you that...' opener, floor anchor fits exactly ('all pieces bury the lede behind context or pleasantries'). Adjusted to score=2 ±2 (allows 0-4) so reasonable graders can score 1-3. structural_economy gets 3 because each piece is technically structured but the 'please advise' close and identical body across all three signals zero adaptation to audience. Tests whether grader correctly catches register failure independent of hedge/apology/lede. Common drift: scoring register higher because each piece individually 'looks professional'.
